sections.paragraph:
συγκοιμάομαι pass. dep. (Aeschyl. et al.) 1 aor. συνεκοιμήθην.

① sleep with τινί someone, freq. as euphemism of intimate sexual relationship (Trag., Hdt.; En 9:8) ApcPt 17:32.

② Before συνεγείρεσθε and after συμπάσχετε, συγκοιμᾶσθε is prob. also a euphemism to lie together in the sleep of death, die together συμπάσχετε, συγκοιμᾶσθε, συνεγείρεσθε suffer together, die together, rise together IPol 6:1 (difft. Lightfoot, to whom such such interp. appears “altogether out of place”: he renders ‘rest together’ [of athletes after vigorous activity]; on the series of compounds w. σύν, among them συγκοιμ., s. Epict. 2, 22, 13 [but here συγκοιμ. of children sleeping together]).

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

dep. dep. = deponent

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

aor. aor. = aorist

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

En En = I Enoch—List 2

ApcPt ApcPt = Apocalypse of Peter—List 1

prob. prob. = probable, probably

IPol IPol = Ignatius to Polycarp—List 1

difft. difft. = different(ly)

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 952
